steps.title: Where Is It? 🧭
steps.desc: Never get lost again! Learn to ask for and give directions in Dutch.

steps.note: 'Links' = left. Same word as 'links' (clumsy). 'Linksaf' = turn left.

steps.exampleEn: The shop is on the right.
steps.note: 'Rechts' = right (direction). 'Recht' = straight. Don't confuse! 'Rechtsaf' = turn right.
Essential direction! 'Rechtdoor' = straight on/ahead. One word, not 'recht door'.
'Om de hoek' = around the corner. 'Op de hoek' = on the corner.

steps.example: Bij het kruispunt ga je links.
steps.exampleEn: At the intersection you go left.
steps.note: 'Kruispunt' = crossing point. Also: 'de kruising' (intersection).
'Bij' = at/by/near (location). 'Bij de supermarkt' = at/by the supermarket.
'Langs' = along/past. 'Langslopen' = to walk past. 'Langs de straat' = along the street.

steps.title: 🗣️ Dialogue: Asking for Directions
steps.text:
A: Pardon, weet u waar het station is?
B: Ja hoor! Ga rechtdoor tot het kruispunt.
A: Oké, en dan?
B: Dan ga je linksaf. Het station is aan je rechterhand.
A: Is het ver lopen?
B: Nee, ongeveer vijf minuten.
A: Perfect, dank u wel!
B: Graag gedaan!

Translation:
A: Excuse me, do you know where the station is?
B: Yes! Go straight until the intersection.
A: Okay, and then?
B: Then you turn left. The station is on your right-hand side.
A: Is it far to walk?
B: No, about five minutes.
A: Perfect, thank you!
B: You're welcome!

steps.title: 🧭 Giving Directions in Dutch
steps.text:
BASIC DIRECTIONS:
⬅️ links — left
➡️ rechts — right
⬆️ rechtdoor — straight ahead
↩️ terug — back

IMPERATIVE (commands):
Ga rechtdoor — Go straight
Neem de eerste straat links — Take the first street on the left
Steek over — Cross (the street)
Loop door — Keep walking

LANDMARKS:
bij het station — at the station
op de hoek — on the corner
om de hoek — around the corner
naast de kerk — next to the church
tegenover de supermarkt — across from the supermarket

FULL EXAMPLE:
'Ga rechtdoor, neem de tweede straat rechts, en het is aan je linkerhand.'
= Go straight, take the second street on the right, and it's on your left-hand side.

steps.title: Public Transport 🚆
steps.desc: Master Dutch public transport! Trains, buses, trams, and the essential OV-chipkaart.

steps.note: 'Het station' (het-word). Amsterdam Centraal is the biggest station. 'NS' = Dutch Railways.
'Halte' = stop (for bus/tram). 'Tramhalte' = tram stop. 'Bushalte' = bus stop.
'De trein' (de-word). 'Met de trein' = by train. Dutch trains are punctual and frequent!

steps.exampleEn: You have to transfer in Utrecht.
steps.note: Separable verb! 'Ik stap over' = I transfer. Essential for journeys with connections.
Separable verb! 'Ik stap uit' = I get off. Opposite: 'instappen' (to get on).
THE Dutch transport card. Check in AND out! 'Inchecken' (check in), 'uitchecken' (check out).
'Perron' = platform. From French 'perron'. 'Spoor' also used for track number.

steps.title: 🗣️ Dialogue: At the Station
steps.text:
A: Hallo, wanneer vertrekt de volgende trein naar Utrecht?
B: Om 14:35, van perron 4.
A: Moet ik overstappen?
B: Nee, het is een directe trein.
A: Perfect! Hoeveel kost een kaartje?
B: Gebruik je OV-chipkaart?
A: Ja.
B: Dan is het ongeveer 8 euro.
A: Oké, dank je! Welk perron was het?
B: Perron 4. Goede reis!

Translation:
A: Hello, when does the next train to Utrecht leave?
B: At 14:35, from platform 4.
A: Do I need to transfer?
B: No, it's a direct train.
A: Perfect! How much does a ticket cost?
B: Are you using your OV-chipkaart?
A: Yes.
B: Then it's about 8 euros.
A: Okay, thanks! Which platform was it?
B: Platform 4. Have a good trip!

steps.title: 🚆 Dutch Public Transport Tips
steps.text:
OV-CHIPKAART RULES:
✅ ALWAYS check in AND out!
✅ Blue poles = check in/out
✅ Yellow readers = balance check
❌ Forget to check out = €20 fine + max fare

ASKING FOR HELP:
'Gaat deze trein naar...?' — Does this train go to...?
'Waar moet ik overstappen?' — Where do I transfer?
'Welk perron?' — Which platform?
'Hoe laat vertrekt de trein?' — What time does the train leave?

TRANSPORT TYPES:
de trein — train (NS)
de tram — tram
de bus — bus
de metro — metro (Amsterdam, Rotterdam)

APPS:
9292.nl — Journey planner (essential!)
NS app — Train times and tickets

Fun fact: Dutch people bike to the station, then take the train. 'Fiets + trein' combo!

steps.s: Vergeet niet om in en uit te _____!

steps.title: By Car & Bike 🚲
steps.desc: The Netherlands is THE cycling country! Learn about bikes, cars, and Dutch traffic culture.

steps.note: THE Dutch icon! 'Op de fiets' = by bike. 'Fietsen' = to cycle. More bikes than people in NL!
Verb: 'Ik fiets, jij fietst'. Dutch people cycle in ALL weather. Rain? No problem!
Sacred in NL! Never walk on the fietspad. Bikes have priority. Red pavement = bike path.
'Auto' = car. 'Met de auto' = by car. Cities are bike-friendly, cars less convenient!
'Parkeren' = to park. Parking in Dutch cities = expensive! €5/hour is normal.

steps.exampleEn: I'm looking for a parking spot.
steps.note: 'Parkeerplaats' = parking spot/lot. Also: 'parkeergarage' (parking garage).

steps.example: Bij het stoplicht ga je rechts.
steps.exampleEn: At the traffic light you go right.
steps.note: 'Stoplicht' = traffic light. Red = stop! But bikes sometimes... bend the rules. 😅

steps.title: 🗣️ Dialogue: Renting a Bike
steps.text:
A: Goedemiddag, ik wil graag een fiets huren.
B: Natuurlijk! Voor hoelang?
A: Eén dag.
B: Dat kan. Heb je een helm nodig?
A: Nee, dank je. Waar kan ik fietsen?
B: Overal! Maar blijf op het fietspad.
A: En waar kan ik de fiets parkeren?
B: Bij elk fietsenrek, maar zet hem op slot!
A: Oké, dank je wel!

Translation:
A: Good afternoon, I'd like to rent a bike.
B: Of course! For how long?
A: One day.
B: That's possible. Do you need a helmet?
A: No, thank you. Where can I cycle?
B: Everywhere! But stay on the bike path.
A: And where can I park the bike?
B: At any bike rack, but lock it!
A: Okay, thank you!

steps.title: 🚲 Dutch Cycling Culture
steps.text:
CYCLING FACTS:
🚲 23 million bikes for 17 million people
🚲 84% of Dutch people own a bike
🚲 27% of ALL trips are by bike
🚲 Amsterdam alone: 881,000 bikes

BIKE VOCABULARY:
de fiets — bike
het fietsenrek — bike rack
het fietsslot — bike lock
de bel — bell (ring ring!)
de ketting — chain
een lekke band — flat tire
de fietsenmaker — bike repair shop

RULES:
✅ Use bike paths (red pavement)
✅ Ring your bell to warn pedestrians
✅ Lock your bike (theft is common!)
✅ Use lights at night (or get fined!)
❌ Don't bike drunk (yes, it's illegal)

Fun: 'Bakfiets' = cargo bike (for kids/groceries)
Dutch parents bike with 3 kids + shopping. It's normal!

CAR FACTS:
Parking is expensive (€3-6/hour)
Many cities have car-free zones
Dutch prefer bikes for short trips
'Park & Ride' = park outside, take tram in

steps.q: What is the bike path called?
What do you MUST have on your bike at night?

steps.title: 🗺️ Transportation Summary
steps.text:
ASKING DIRECTIONS:
Waar is...? — Where is...?
Hoe kom ik bij...? — How do I get to...?
Is het ver? — Is it far?
Kan ik lopen? — Can I walk?

GIVING DIRECTIONS:
Ga rechtdoor — Go straight
Neem de eerste/tweede straat links/rechts
Steek over — Cross
Het is aan je linkerhand — It's on your left

TRANSPORT PHRASES:
Ik ga met de trein/bus/fiets/auto
Ik neem de trein
Ik fiets erheen (I bike there)
Hoe laat vertrekt...? — What time does... leave?
Moet ik overstappen? — Do I need to transfer?

PARKING:
Waar kan ik parkeren?
Is er een parkeergarage?
Hoeveel kost parkeren? — How much is parking?
